notes.anchor: the Roman people would make you consul with the highest enthusiasm and by every vote
notes.note: The two consuls were elected each year by the comitia centuriata, the citizen assembly voting in property-graded units. Paullus has just been returned as one of the consuls-designate for 50 BC, the news of which Cicero is celebrating from Cilicia.

notes.anchor: the supreme distinction of your family
notes.note: L. Aemilius Paullus belonged to one of Rome's most illustrious patrician houses, the Aemilii Paulli, whose ancestors included the victor of Pydna; the family pedigree was itself a strong electoral asset.

notes.anchor: no time be added to my year-long commission
notes.note: Cicero's governorship of Cilicia was fixed by law at a single year, but a senatorial decree could prolong it; he repeatedly begs his correspondents to ensure his term is not extended.
